title: Annotate ggplot2 Visualisation
text: objective ggscribe provide helpers annotate ‘ggplot2’ Visualisation

title: Annotate ggplot2 Visualisation
text: Install CRAN, development version GitHub.

title: Annotate ggplot2 Visualisation
text: ggscribe provides annotations like :

what: A mapped aesthetic for text colour on fill — aes_contrast
title: A mapped aesthetic for text colour on fill — aes_contrast
text: Modifies mapped colour (fill) aesthetic contrast fill (colour) aesthetic. Function can spliced ggplot2::aes rlang::!!!.

title: A mapped aesthetic for text colour on fill — aes_contrast
code: aes_contrast(..., dark = NULL, light = NULL, aesthetic = "colour")

title: A mapped aesthetic for text colour on fill — aes_contrast
text: ... Require named arguments (support trailing commas). dark dark colour. NULL, derived theme text panel background. light light colour. NULL, derived theme text panel background. aesthetic aesthetic modified contrast. Either "colour" (default) "fill".

title: A mapped aesthetic for text colour on fill — aes_contrast
text: ggplot2 aesthetic ggplot2::aes.

title: A mapped aesthetic for text colour on fill — aes_contrast
code: library(ggplot2) library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: ‘dplyr’ #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:stats’: #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’: #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union library(stringr)  set_theme(  ggrefine::theme_light(     panel_heights = rep(unit(50, "mm"), 100),     panel_widths = rep(unit(75, "mm"), 100),  ) )  ggwidth::set_equiwidth(equiwidth = 1.75)  mtcars |>   count(cyl, am) |>   mutate(     am = if_else(am == 0, "Automatic", "Manual"),     cyl = as.factor(cyl)   ) |>   ggplot(aes(x = am, y = n, colour = cyl, fill = cyl, label = n)) +   geom_col(     position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single", padding = 0.05),     width = ggwidth::get_width(n = 2, n_dodge = 3),   ) +   scale_fill_discrete(palette = jumble::jumble) +   scale_colour_discrete(palette = blends::multiply(jumble::jumble)) +   geom_text(     mapping = ggscribe::aes_contrast(), # or aes(!!!ggscribe::aes_contrast()),     position = position_dodge2(       width = ggwidth::get_width(n = 2, n_dodge = 3),       padding = 0.05,       preserve = "single"),     vjust = 1.33,     show.legend = FALSE,   ) +   scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(c(0, 0.05))) +   ggrefine::refine_modern(x_type = "discrete")   mtcars |>   count(cyl, am) |>   mutate(     am = if_else(am == 0, "automatic", "manual"),     am = stringr::str_to_sentence(am),     cyl = as.factor(cyl)   ) |>   ggplot(aes(y = am, x = n, colour = cyl, fill = cyl, label = n)) +   geom_col(     position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single", padding = 0.05),     width = ggwidth::get_width(n = 2, n_dodge = 3, orientation = "y"),   ) +   scale_fill_discrete(palette = jumble::jumble) +   scale_colour_discrete(palette = blends::multiply(jumble::jumble)) +   geom_text(     mapping = ggscribe::aes_contrast(), # or aes(!!!ggscribe::aes_contrast()),     position = position_dodge2(       width = ggwidth::get_width(n = 2, n_dodge = 3, orientation = "y"),       preserve = "single",       padding = 0.05,     ),     hjust = 1.25,     show.legend = FALSE,   ) +   scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(c(0, 0.05))) +   ggrefine::refine_modern(y_type = "discrete")

title: Annotate an axis bracket — annotate_axis_bracket
text: Draws bracket spanning min(breaks) max(breaks) along axis edge, optional centered label. Style defaults taken axis.line axis.text elements set theme. Requires coord_cartesian(clip = "").

title: Annotate an axis bracket — annotate_axis_bracket
code: annotate_axis_bracket(   ...,   position = NULL,   breaks,   labels = NULL,   colour = NULL,   size = NULL,   family = NULL,   linewidth = NULL,   linetype = NULL,   tick_length = NULL,   xintercept = NULL,   yintercept = NULL,   elements_to = "transparent" )

title: Annotate an axis bracket — annotate_axis_bracket
text: ... used. Allows trailing commas named-argument style calls. position One "top", "bottom", "left", "right". breaks numeric vector length >= 2. bracket spans min(breaks) max(breaks). labels optional label string (function returning one) centered bracket. Defaults NULL (label). colour Inherits axis.line set theme. size Label text size. Inherits axis.text set theme. family Label font family. Inherits axis.text set theme. linewidth Inherits axis.line set theme. Supports rel(). linetype Inherits axis.line set theme. tick_length Length bracket end caps grid unit. Supports rel(). Defaults theme tick length. xintercept "left"/"right" axes: float bracket x position data coordinates instead panel edge. yintercept "top"/"bottom" axes: float bracket y position data coordinates instead panel edge. elements_to One "keep", "transparent", "blank". Controls whether native theme ticks suppressed. Defaults "transparent".

title: Annotate an axis bracket — annotate_axis_bracket
text: list ggplot2 annotation layers theme elements.

title: Annotate an axis line — annotate_axis_line
text: Draws line along axis edge, style defaults taken axis.line element set theme. Requires coord_cartesian(clip = "").

title: Annotate an axis line — annotate_axis_line
code: annotate_axis_line(   ...,   position = NULL,   breaks = NULL,   colour = NULL,   linewidth = NULL,   linetype = NULL,   xintercept = NULL,   yintercept = NULL,   elements_to = "transparent" )

title: Annotate an axis line — annotate_axis_line
text: ... used. Allows trailing commas named-argument style calls. position One "top", "bottom", "left", "right". breaks Optional numeric vector length 2 specifying c(, ) draw partial line. Defaults full axis extent. colour Inherits axis.line set theme. linewidth Inherits axis.line set theme. Supports rel(). linetype Inherits axis.line set theme. xintercept "left"/"right" axes: float axis x position data coordinates instead panel edge. yintercept "top"/"bottom" axes: float axis y position data coordinates instead panel edge. elements_to One "keep", "transparent", "blank". Controls whether native theme axis line suppressed. Defaults "transparent".

title: Annotate an axis line — annotate_axis_line
text: list ggplot2 annotation layers theme elements.

title: Annotate axis text — annotate_axis_text
text: Draws text labels specified break positions along axis, style defaults taken axis.text element set theme. Requires coord_cartesian(clip = "").

title: Annotate axis text — annotate_axis_text
code: annotate_axis_text(   ...,   position = NULL,   breaks,   labels = NULL,   colour = NULL,   size = NULL,   family = NULL,   tick_length = NULL,   hjust = NULL,   vjust = NULL,   angle = 0,   xintercept = NULL,   yintercept = NULL,   elements_to = "transparent" )

title: Annotate axis text — annotate_axis_text
text: ... used. Allows trailing commas named-argument style calls. position One "top", "bottom", "left", "right". breaks numeric vector break positions. labels One : NULL (default) auto-format break values character vector length breaks function taking break values returning labels colour Inherits axis.text set theme. size Inherits axis.text set theme. family Inherits axis.text set theme. tick_length Offset axis edge including tick length margin. Supports rel(). Negative values place labels inside panel. hjust, vjust Justification. Auto-calculated position NULL. angle Text rotation angle. Defaults 0. xintercept "left"/"right" axes: float axis x position data coordinates instead panel edge. yintercept "top"/"bottom" axes: float axis y position data coordinates instead panel edge. elements_to One "keep", "transparent", "blank". Controls whether native theme axis text suppressed. Defaults "transparent".

title: Annotate axis text — annotate_axis_text
text: list ggplot2 annotation layers theme elements.

title: Annotate axis ticks — annotate_axis_ticks
text: Draws axis ticks specified break positions, style defaults taken axis.ticks element set theme. Requires coord_cartesian(clip = "").

title: Annotate axis ticks — annotate_axis_ticks
code: annotate_axis_ticks(   ...,   position = NULL,   breaks,   minor = FALSE,   colour = NULL,   linewidth = NULL,   tick_length = NULL,   xintercept = NULL,   yintercept = NULL,   elements_to = "transparent" )

title: Annotate axis ticks — annotate_axis_ticks
text: ... used. Allows trailing commas named-argument style calls. position One "top", "bottom", "left", "right". breaks numeric vector break positions. minor Logical. TRUE, uses minor tick theme defaults. Defaults FALSE. colour Inherits axis.ticks set theme. linewidth Inherits axis.ticks set theme. Supports rel(). tick_length Total tick length grid unit. Supports rel() scale relative theme default. Negative values flip tick direction. xintercept "left"/"right" axes: float axis x position data coordinates instead panel edge. yintercept "top"/"bottom" axes: float axis y position data coordinates instead panel edge. elements_to One "keep", "transparent", "blank". Controls whether native theme ticks suppressed. Defaults "transparent".

title: Annotate axis ticks — annotate_axis_ticks
text: list ggplot2 annotation layers theme elements.

what: Annotate a shaded panel region — annotate_panel_shade
title: Annotate a shaded panel region — annotate_panel_shade
text: Draws filled rectangle panel colour defaults taken set theme. Defaults subtle overlay across full panel, fill automatically adapting light dark panel backgrounds.

title: Annotate a shaded panel region — annotate_panel_shade
code: annotate_panel_shade(   ...,   xmin = -Inf,   xmax = Inf,   ymin = -Inf,   ymax = Inf,   fill = "#878580",   alpha = 0.25,   colour = "transparent",   linewidth = NULL,   linetype = NULL )

title: Annotate a shaded panel region — annotate_panel_shade
text: ... used. Allows trailing commas named-argument style calls. xmin, xmax Left right edges rectangle. Defaults -Inf Inf. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). ymin, ymax Bottom top edges rectangle. Defaults -Inf Inf. Use () normalized coordinates (0-1). fill Fill colour. Defaults neutral grey. alpha Opacity rectangle. Defaults 0.25. colour Border colour. Defaults "transparent". linewidth Inherits panel.border set theme. Supports rel(). linetype Border linetype. Defaults 1.

title: Annotate a shaded panel region — annotate_panel_shade
text: list containing annotation layer.

title: Annotate a shaded panel region — annotate_panel_shade
code: library(ggplot2)  set_theme(   ggrefine::theme_grey(     panel_heights = rep(unit(50, "mm"), 100),     panel_widths = rep(unit(75, "mm"), 100),    )  )  p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point()  # Shade the full panel p + annotate_panel_shade()   # Shade a specific data range p + annotate_panel_shade(xmin = 3, xmax = 4)   # Shade using normalized coordinates p + annotate_panel_shade(xmin = I(0.25), xmax = I(0.75))   # Custom fill and opacity p + annotate_panel_shade(ymin = 20, ymax = 30, fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.15)

title: Annotate a reference line — annotate_reference_line
text: Draws reference line within inside panel, style defaults taken axis.line element set theme (apart linetype, defaults "dashed"). Requires coord_cartesian(clip = "").

title: Annotate a reference line — annotate_reference_line
code: annotate_reference_line(   ...,   breaks = NULL,   colour = NULL,   linewidth = NULL,   linetype = "dashed",   xintercept = NULL,   yintercept = NULL )

title: Annotate a reference line — annotate_reference_line
text: ... used. Allows trailing commas named-argument style calls. breaks Optional numeric vector length 2 specifying c(, ) draw partial line. Defaults full axis extent. colour Inherits axis.line set theme. linewidth Inherits axis.line set theme. Supports rel(). linetype Inherits axis.line set theme. xintercept Draw vertical reference line x position. yintercept Draw horizontal reference line y position.

title: Annotate a reference line — annotate_reference_line
text: list ggplot2 annotation layers.

what: Duplicate axis with axis text only — dup_axis_text
title: Duplicate axis with axis text only — dup_axis_text
text: wrapper around ggplot2::dup_axis() creates secondary axis displaying axis text — axis lines ticks hidden, making useful placing annotation labels (e.g. region labels alongside shaded panel).

title: Duplicate axis with axis text only — dup_axis_text
code: dup_axis_text(   breaks = ggplot2::waiver(),   labels = ggplot2::derive(),   elements_to = "transparent",   ... )

title: Duplicate axis with axis text only — dup_axis_text
text: breaks One : NULL breaks ggplot2::waiver() (default) inherit breaks primary axis numeric vector break positions function takes scale limits input returns break positions (e.g. \(x) mean(c(x[2], 32))) labels One : ggplot2::derive() (default) derive labels breaks character vector labels, length breaks function takes break positions input returns labels elements_to One "keep", "transparent", "blank". Controls whether native theme ticks suppressed. Defaults "keep". ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::dup_axis().

title: Duplicate axis with axis text only — dup_axis_text
text: AxisSecondary object use sec.axis argument scale_x_continuous() scale_y_continuous().

title: Duplicate axis with axis text only — dup_axis_text
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(mpg, aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) +    geom_point() +    annotate_panel_shade(ymin = 32) +    scale_y_continuous(      sec.axis = dup_axis_text(        breaks = \(x) mean(c(x[2], 32)),        labels = "Inefficient",      )    )

title: ggscribe: Annotate 'ggplot2' Visualisation — ggscribe-package
text: Annotation helpers 'ggplot2' visualisations, style defaults inherited active theme. includes annotation functions cover axis lines, ticks, text, panel grid lines, shaded regions. makes straightforward annotate plots way stays consistent set theme.

title: ggscribe: Annotate 'ggplot2' Visualisation — ggscribe-package
